other_observers: There have been nearly 140 observers who all have seen the bird. If you want a complete list I will email it.; Original finder: Al Kogler; he did not know what it was, I assumed it was a Bullock's Oriole. Rachel asked if I'd ruled out SBOR and when I checked Sibley's, I realized it was a SBOR. Rachel's response to my incorrect Bullocks Ori
duration: This bird has been observed almost continually since start time.
specific_location: In the front and back yard of my home 4778 Hay Wagon Ct. Loveland, Co.
description: This bird has all the field marks of a Streak-backed Oriole; Dark orange, almost reddish malar, finely streaked back, bright orange belly goes all the way down past the legs, bright orange/yellow undertail coverts. Deep black mask with no line behind the eye. Lower mandible dark tipped, beak large. Short primary projection.White tipped lesser and greater coverts.Behaviors: This bird was first seen eating roasted peanut halves. Over the course of the last 3 days it has tried every food at the feeder, eating mostly grape jelly, suet pellets and peanuts. Roosts deep in a spruce tree or juniper tree. Likes feeding when feeders are not too busy.Call: Not heardPlumage: First Year
photographer: Photographer: Connie Kogler, Connie Kogler, Connie Kogler, Connie Kogler, Connie Kogler
